space.title: The Sun
space.content: The Sun is a giant star at the center of our solar system. It is so big that one million Earths could fit inside it! The Sun gives us light and warmth every day.
space.funFact: The Sun is about 4.6 billion years old!

space.title: Planet Earth
space.content: Earth is our home planet. It is the only planet we know of with liquid water on its surface. Earth has one moon that orbits around it.
space.funFact: Earth spins around once every 24 hours!

space.title: Stars in the Sky
space.content: Stars are huge balls of hot gas that produce light and heat. On a clear night, you can see thousands of stars from Earth. Our Sun is actually a star, just much closer to us than the others.
space.funFact: There are more stars in the universe than grains of sand on all Earth's beaches!

animals.title: Lions
animals.content: Lions are big cats that live in Africa. They live in groups called prides. Male lions have a big mane of fur around their face. Lions are very fast runners.
animals.funFact: A lion's roar can be heard from 8 kilometres away!

animals.title: Elephants
animals.content: Elephants are the largest land animals on Earth. They use their long trunks to pick up food and water. Baby elephants stay close to their mothers for many years.
animals.funFact: Elephants never forget! They have amazing memories.

animals.title: Penguins
animals.content: Penguins are birds that cannot fly, but they are excellent swimmers. They live mostly in the cold southern hemisphere, especially Antarctica. Penguins huddle together to stay warm in cold weather.
animals.funFact: Emperor penguins can dive up to 500 metres deep!

dinosaurs.title: T-Rex
dinosaurs.content: Tyrannosaurus Rex was one of the biggest meat-eating dinosaurs. It had tiny arms but very strong legs. T-Rex had huge sharp teeth perfect for eating other dinosaurs.
dinosaurs.funFact: T-Rex teeth were as long as bananas!

dinosaurs.title: Triceratops
dinosaurs.content: Triceratops had three horns on its face and a big frill around its head. It was a plant-eater and used its horns to protect itself. Triceratops was about the size of an elephant.

dinosaurs.title: Pterodactyl
dinosaurs.content: Pterodactyls were flying reptiles that lived at the same time as dinosaurs. They were not actually dinosaurs themselves! They had large wings made of skin and could soar high in the sky.
dinosaurs.funFact: Some pterodactyls had wingspans as wide as a small plane!

ocean.title: Dolphins
ocean.content: Dolphins are very smart sea animals. They live in groups called pods and love to play. Dolphins breathe air just like us and must come to the surface to breathe.
ocean.funFact: Dolphins sleep with one eye open!

ocean.title: Sharks
ocean.content: Sharks are powerful fish that have lived in the ocean for millions of years. They have rows of sharp teeth and a very good sense of smell. Most sharks are not dangerous to people.
ocean.funFact: A shark can have up to 30,000 teeth in its lifetime!

ocean.title: Coral Reefs
ocean.content: Coral reefs are underwater structures made by tiny animals called coral polyps. They are sometimes called the rainforests of the sea because so many animals live there. Coral reefs need warm, clean water to survive.
ocean.funFact: The Great Barrier Reef in Australia can be seen from space!

sports.title: Football (Soccer)
sports.content: Football is the most popular sport in the world. Two teams of eleven players try to kick a ball into the other team's goal. Matches are played for 90 minutes in two halves.
sports.funFact: The first Football World Cup was held in Uruguay in 1930!

sports.title: Swimming
sports.content: Swimming is a sport where you move through water using your arms and legs. There are four main swimming strokes: freestyle, backstroke, breaststroke, and butterfly. Swimming is also great exercise for your whole body.
sports.funFact: Humans can swim as fast as 8.6 km per hour!

sports.title: Cricket
sports.content: Cricket is a team sport played with a bat and ball. Two teams of eleven players take turns batting and fielding. The batting team tries to score runs while the fielding team tries to get the batters out.
sports.funFact: A cricket ball can travel at over 150 km per hour!

music.title: Drums
music.content: Drums are musical instruments you hit to make sounds. They are one of the oldest instruments in the world. Drums help set the beat in a band and make music fun to dance to.
music.funFact: The world's largest drum is taller than a two-storey building!

music.title: Piano
music.content: The piano has black and white keys that you press to make sounds. When you press a key, a small hammer inside hits a string to create a note. A full piano has 88 keys.
music.funFact: Beethoven composed music even after he went deaf!

music.title: How Sound Works
music.content: Sound is made when something vibrates, or shakes very fast. These vibrations travel through the air as waves and reach our ears. The faster the vibrations, the higher the pitch of the sound.
music.funFact: Sound travels about 343 metres every second in air!
